ops.title: P3 primitives rulings: R-E skip-carry equivalence, R-F manual coverage, R-G/R-H scout
ops.body:
Binding rulings from the P3 primitives wave (shipped 523974f83df4), each earned through adversarial rounds with executed repros:

**R-E — skip-carry equivalence (optional legs).** For every variant plan V obtained by removing any subset of `optional` legs from P, `compile_graph(P)`'s coverage per shared leg — origin sets AND date windows AND cash gateway dates — is a superset of `compile_graph(V)`'s. Journeys' runtime chaining over V's intents is the ground truth the compile layer serves. Mechanism: optional legs are transparent to downstream endpoint derivation via `endpoint_source.skip_sources` (a skipped leg's declared origins vanish with it — the skip source is always the pre-boundary, folding recursively through consecutive optionals); the window envelope applies at `_leg_window`'s single exit (every branch, hard trip caps clamp after); one shared `_skip_source_window` derivation serves the sweep and cash lanes. Pinned as a parametrized equivalence matrix (test_sweeps.py) that literally compiles variant plans. Three failed shortcuts are on record: override-as-skip-source, stay-branch-only envelope, raw-trip-window cash dates.

**R-F — manual-chain coverage.** A `legs/manual.json` chain covers every MANDATORY leg exactly once in plan order and may include or skip optional legs; its leg subset is its VARIANT, threaded through composition/fit like `_leg_variants` variants. R-D applies (no opening on the homeward `$origins` leg — honest rejection, never the fit IndexError). Both boundaries (write validator, read-time re-check) enforce the same rule. Manual is an INPUT artifact: re-validates at READ time against the current plan (per-chain manual_rejected reasons), never fingerprint-invalidates.

**R-G — scout read-time re-validation.** `_scout_endpoints` re-validates scout.json against the CURRENT discover spec at read (reusing the write-boundary validator): spec violation or any parse failure (JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError, zero-byte) → typed UsageError naming the remedy; absent file or `[]` → typed NoData. A zero-byte file is corruption, NOT an empty scout — NoData there would masquerade corruption as an empty market.

**R-H — scout is additive.** Discover coexists with buckets/program_sweeps on the same leg: bucket sweeps + program availability sweeps + the scout-fed bare sweep compile side by side; shortlist merges across all of a leg's sweeps. Scout adds endpoints, never gates. Only the bare sweep declares scout.json as an input (a bucket sweep never re-runs on scout re-runs — quota honesty).

**Standing rejections re-affirmed:** per-step beam pruning in `_build_chains` (accepted counterexample: cannot match whole-chain top-k; Eppstein k-best is the escalation if fanout ever bites). **Adjudicated nits (recorded, not fixed):** manual chain duplicating a composed chain computes fit/_cost twice before id-dedupe (pure local, one consistent artifact); `iso_date` trailing-newline unpinned (fromisoformat backstops).

Also fixed in this wave: every `$`-anchored `.match()` validation in trips.py went `fullmatch` — Python's `$` matches before a terminal newline, so `'NRT\n'` passed IATA validation and reached HTTP call sites.
